response.justification: While Smith is an accomplished software engineer with 15+ years of experience, this embedded security/networking role requires domain expertise he lacks. Most critically, native-level Japanese proficiency is a stated requirement—he speaks only English, Romanian, and basic German. His career has focused on web platforms and high-level services, not firmware, security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, encryption), or embedded networking (L2/3, NETCONF, SNMP). The combination of missing language skills and fundamental domain mismatch makes this a poor fit.
strengths: 15+ years software development across multiple domains with strong fundamentals in Linux, C/C++, and software craftsmanship (TDD, CI/CD)
Demonstrated systems programming and embedded experience including work at National Instruments on measurement IDEs and infrastructure tooling
Proven ability to master new languages and technologies quickly, with proficiency in C, C++, Java, and multiple modern frameworks
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency listed; role requires native-level fluency as a stated requirement
Career trajectory shows clear shift away from C toward high-level languages (TypeScript, Node.js); most recent roles lack embedded systems and firmware focus
No demonstrated expertise in required security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, encryption, tamper detection) or embedded networking (L2/3 protocols, NETCONF, SNMP, switch operation)
